arts.ttl: Team News: Chelsea vs. Arsenal injury, suspension list, predicted XIs
arts.sum: Sports Mole rounds up all of the latest injury and suspension news ahead of Sunday's Premier League clash between Chelsea and Arsenal.

arts.ttl: Chelsea's dream Alexander Isak transfer scenario emerges as Arsenal learn brutal reality
arts.sum: Chelsea have been handed a potential boost in their reported pursuit of signing Newcastle striker Alexander Isak at the expense of Premier League rivals Arsenal. It has been reported that Isak has a desire to play Champions League football, which could see the striker seeking a move away from Newcastle if the club do not qualify for the competition next season.

arts.ttl: Chelsea could finally get dream Kylian Mbappe transfer opportunity
arts.sum: Kylian Mbappe’s struggles at Real Madrid could be good news for Chelsea’ s hopes of signing the player in the near future, with the Blues previously linked with an interest in the French star. In the summer of 2023, the Times reported that Chelsea were one of the clubs showing an interest in buying Mbappe from PSG.

arts.ttl: Every Player Who Played for Arsenal and Chelsea in the Premier League (Ranked)
arts.sum: Ranking every player to have played for Arsenal and Chelsea in the Premier League era, including Jorginho, Kai Havertz and Cesc Fabregas

arts.ttl: EXCLUSIVE: Nelson explores Bundesliga move amid Arsenal frustration
arts.sum: According to sources, Nelson has reached out to Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta, expressing his desire for a permanent transfer to a club where he can truly establish himself following his loan at Fulham . It is a continuation of his struggles at Arsenal where he never became more than a squad player despite having shown promise out on loan at Hoffenheim in 2018/19 and Feyenoord in 2021/22.

arts.ttl: Declan Rice delivers final Arsenal red card verdict as clear promise made before Chelsea
arts.sum: Declan Rice has offered his thoughts on Arsenal's three red cards picked up so far this season before their Premier League match against Chelsea. The latter is causing Arteta the most headaches for this weekend's impending London derby with Rice, Kai Havertz and Mikel Merino all still fitness doubts.

arts.ttl: Chelsea predicted lineup vs Arsenal - Premier League
arts.sum: Predicting how Chelsea could line up against Arsenal in Sunday's London derby in the Premier League.

arts.ttl: "Under one condition" - Christopher Nkunku's January Chelsea exit stance 'revealed'
arts.sum: Christopher Nkunku is reportedly not planning to leave Chelsea in January but may consider doing so unless there is a change in his situation by next summer.

arts.ttl: Barcelona 'hatch swap deal plan' to beat Chelsea to Karim Adeyemi transfer next summer
arts.sum: Barcelona could offer Spanish attacker Ferran Torres to Borussia Dortmund as part of a potential deal for Karim Adeyemi, according to a report.

arts.ttl: Saliba was Arsenal scouting success that Chelsea spent huge sum to match
arts.sum: The Gunners got in early to land William Saliba for £25m – Chelsea later signed his teammate Wesley Fofana for £70m It was a cold, rainy afternoon in Montpellier when Arsenal first became serious about William Saliba. This was 2019, before the scouts, agents and rival clubs would descend en masse. He was playing for Saint-Étienne’s under-19s and Ty Gooden, then Arsenal’s France scout, had alerted them to the raw yet impressive 6ft 4in 18-year-old working his way through the ranks. His insistence had brought Francis Cagigao, then the club’s head of scouting, to the south of France. Cagigao and Gooden initially had their eye on another centre-half at Saint-Étienne, a year older and already in the first team. He will also be playing on Sunday at Stamford Bridge, against Saliba. Indeed, the older centre-half would eventually command a £70m transfer fee. However, it was Saliba’s extraordinary speed and presence that attracted the Arsenal duo over Wesley Fofana.
